stories.headline: Belarus stages nuclear drills with Russia as Kyiv warns of new offensive
stories.summary: Russia and Belarus conducted joint nuclear exercises on Monday as Kyiv warned Moscow was preparing a new offensive from Belarusian territory against Ukraine or NATO.

stories.headline: Belarus dismisses concerns as it launches joint nuclear drills with Russia
stories.summary: Strong reactions from Ukraine and NATO allies as Belarusian and Russian forces train to use nuclear weapons.

stories.headline: Belarus Starts Nuclear Drills With Russia Amid Kyiv Warnings
stories.summary: Belarus announced that its armed forces would conduct snap nuclear exercises alongside Russian allies, following warnings from Kyiv that it could retaliate if Minsk becomes more deeply involved in the Kremlin’s war.

stories.headline: Lebanese president vows to 'do the impossible' to end war with Israel as strikes continue despite ceasefire
stories.summary: On Monday, the Lebanese health ministry said the death toll from Israeli strikes since the start of the war had reached 3,020, with 211 people 18 and under and 116 healthcare workers among those killed.

stories.headline: Four killed in protests during Kenyan strikes over high fuel prices
stories.summary: Thousands of commuters are stranded, with key roads in the capital Nairobi largely empty due to the protests over fuel costs.

stories.headline: Protests over fuel price hikes turn deadly in Kenya
stories.summary: Several people have been killed in protest violence in Kenya where transport workers blocked roads.

stories.headline: UN calls on Israel to prevent Gaza ‘genocide’

stories.headline: Magyar signals first openness to Ukraine accession talks since Orbán’s departure
stories.summary: Hungary's new government says it is open to engaging with Ukraine on its accession to the EU, according to several sources. Meanwhile, a technical dialogue with Kyiv has been announced on the thorny issue of Hungarian minorities. Brussels looks for a breakthrough.

stories.headline: Infectious diseases such as hantavirus and Ebola becoming more frequent and damaging, say experts

stories.headline: Cuba warns US of ‘bloodbath’ if military action follows drone claims
stories.summary: Cuba’s president, Miguel Díaz-Canel, says any US strike would be catastrophic after reports of 300+ dronesCuba’s president, Miguel Díaz-Canel, has warned that any US military action against his country would lead to a “bloodbath” with incalculable consequences for regional peace and stability.“Cuba does not represent a threat,” Díaz-Canel said in a post on X. stories.headline: 'No society is immune' to housing crisis, UN chief warns at World Urban Forum in Baku
stories.summary: António Guterres told the World Urban Forum in Baku that no country is immune from the housing crisis, as 40,000 delegates gathered for the second day of talks.

stories.headline: Teargas fired as protests erupt over rising fuel prices in Kenya
stories.summary: Police fired teargas at protesters in Nairobi as demonstrations broke out during a nationwide transport strike over soaring fuel prices and rising living costs.

stories.headline: UAE still investigating mystery nuclear plant drone attack
stories.summary: The enigma surrounding Sunday’s unprecedented drone attack on the Barakah nuclear plant deepened on Monday as the UAE said the drone came from its western border, but it did not accuse Iran.

stories.headline: Trump says he called off new Iran attack at request of Gulf states
stories.summary: The US president says he is holding off on a US attack planned for Tuesday as "serious negotiations are now taking place".

stories.headline: Trump warns 'clock is ticking' for Iran as peace progress stalls

stories.headline: 'Everest Man' and 'Mountain Queen' break own records scaling world's tallest peak

stories.headline: US Natural Gas Futures Top $3 for First Time Since March
stories.summary: US natural gas futures ended higher as forecasts shifted to show hotter weather in the coming weeks, signaling increased demand for the power-plant fuel as homes and businesses crank up air-conditioners. Intraday and end-of-day settlement prices for the front-month contract reached more than $3 per million British thermal units for the first time since late March.

stories.headline: SpaceX IPO Adds Second Musk Stock. It’s a Problem for Tesla
stories.summary: For years, there was only one way for mom-and-pop investors to buy into Elon Musk’s vision: shares of Tesla Inc. That’s about to change — and it’s a serious risk for Tesla investors.

stories.headline: US Regulators Poised to Reshape Secret Ratings Process for Banks
stories.summary: US regulators are poised to unveil as soon as this week a plan that would overhaul how examiners secretly rate banks, according to people familiar with the matter, a move likely to be cheered by lenders.

stories.headline: Mexico Signs Deal for Floating Gas Plant to Meet Yucatan Demand
stories.summary: Mexico signed an agreement to send a floating gas power plant to the Yucatan peninsula, the latest in a series of private partnerships aiming to boost electricity generation and shore up the nation’s grid against seasonal blackouts.

stories.headline: Blue Owl Fund Returns to Market With $400 Million Bond Offering
stories.summary: A Blue Owl Capital Inc. private credit fund sold a $400 million investment-grade bond, a month after a debt sale to bond giant Pacific Investment Management Co. helped ease capital-access worries.

stories.headline: Eager for Arms Deal, Taiwan Stresses Need for U.S. Support

stories.headline: At least eight people killed in Bangkok rail crossing collision

stories.headline: Rescue diver dies during search for bodies of Italians who drowned in Maldives caves
stories.summary: Staff Sgt Mohamed Mahdhee died while diving in the search-and-rescue operation, the Maldives government tells the BBC.
